entities.name: GENDER
entities.description: A social, structural, and institutional factor influencing water distribution, access, and decision-making

entities.name: WATER GOVERNANCE
entities.description: The system of policies, plans, and institutional arrangements governing water use and distribution

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: The country where the water governance and gender study is conducted

entities.name: LAIKIPIA
entities.description: The specific region in Kenya where community water groups are analyzed

entities.name: GENDER INEQUALITY
entities.description: The disparity between men and women regarding access to water and decision-making power

entities.name: GENDER ANALYTICAL FRAMEWORK
entities.description: A tool used to identify levels of engagement in gender mainstreaming and the reduction of inequality

entities.name: GENDER MAINSTREAMING
entities.description: A strategy of integrating a gender perspective into all policies and programs

entities.name: TWO-THIRDS GENDER RULE
entities.description: A structural policy measure limiting the representation of any one gender to two-thirds in governance arrangements

entities.name: SOCIO-CULTURAL BELIEFS AND NORMS
entities.description: Social factors that can hinder gender-equitable access to water

entities.name: FINANCIAL CAPABILITY
entities.description: The economic capacity of individuals which may influence water access more strongly than gender

entities.name: COMMUNITY WATER GROUPS
entities.description: Local organizations that manage water and implement governance rules at the community level

entities.name: WATER ACCESS
entities.description: The ability of individuals to obtain and use water resources

entities.name: DECISION MAKING
entities.description: The process of making choices regarding the use and management of water

relationships.description: is a level of engagement within
